expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly enumerates the case focus and then covers all three elements under “Context intake extracted”: (A) “Component-stack constraints captured” (labels Embedding_SDK, Library_and_Dashboards), (B) “Embedding lifecycle assumptions captured” (native-embedding + SDK lifecycle concerns), and (C) “Integration-sensitive customer expectations captured” (customer signal true; “CVS Pharmacy / CS0928640”).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output frames itself as Phase 1 and stays within Phase 1 contract discussion: “Primary phase under test: Phase 1” and “Phase 1 is a spawn-manifest generation checkpoint.” It also avoids later-phase drafting/review and explicitly notes limits: “cannot be verified… no produced phase1_spawn_manifest.json… no Phase 1 --post validation output,” which is consistent with Phase 1 alignment under blind_pre_defect evidence constraints.
